title_en: Streamer IShowSpeed Is Gen Z’s ESPN
title_zh: 直播主 IShowSpeed 成为了 Z 世代的 ESPN
rubric_en: At 21, Speed has pushed the limits of streaming by transforming a distinctly solo format into a global group chat. His song for this year’s World Cup is becoming the tournament’s unofficial anthem.
rubric_zh: 在21岁时，Speed 把直播的上限推到了极致，他把一种明显偏向单人直播的形式，变成了一个全球性的群聊空间。今年世界杯期间，他的那首歌正逐渐成为比赛的非官方圣歌。

paragraphs.en: In the days leading up to the 2026 World Cup, the streamer IShowSpeed—one of the most watched people on the planet, who occasionally moonlights as a rapper—released the music video “World Cup (Champions),” a song about flexing national pride where he mentions all 48 teams.
paragraphs.zh: 在2026年世界杯开赛前的几天，直播主 IShowSpeed——这位地球上最受关注的人物之一，偶尔也会兼做饶舌歌手——发布了音乐视频《World Cup (Champions)》，这是一首充满民族自豪感的歌曲，他在歌中提到了全部48支球队。

paragraphs.en: As with everything the 21-year-old born Darren Watkins Jr. does, the video was instantly everywhere. The song racked up over 7 million views on YouTube in under 24 hours. The internet rushed to christen it as the anthem of the tournament, even though the World Cup already has one. FIFA, following a ridiculous outpouring from fans and perhaps realizing the massive instant exposure he could bring, added the song to its official album.
paragraphs.zh: 和这位21岁主播达伦·沃特金斯二世（Darren Watkins Jr.）的每次发布一样，这支视频发布后瞬间铺天盖地。短短24小时内，这首歌在 YouTube 上就累积超过700万次观看。尽管世界杯早已有官方颂歌，网络却仍迅速把它奉为本届赛事的“应援圣歌”。FIFA 在粉丝疯狂反响中，也许意识到他能带来的巨大即时曝光后，把这首歌收录进了官方专辑。

paragraphs.en: That is the power of Speed—a one-man, always-on media network.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是 Speed 的影响力——一台只靠一个人、但始终在线的媒体网络。

paragraphs.en: Speed started posting on YouTube as a teenager in 2017. But it wasn’t until the Covid-19 lockdowns, in 2020, that the Cincinnati, Ohio, native committed to streaming full-time. His passion for soccer, particularly his admiration for his favorite player, Cristiano Ronaldo, shaped his earliest broadcasts, where he played FIFA and NBA 2K as people watched along. His virality came thanks to the kind of scream-laden occasionally controversial antics the internet loves, but over time Speed ditched the stereotypical streamer playbook. The future of his brand existed outside of his bedroom.
paragraphs.zh: Speed 在2017年作为一名青少年开始在YouTube上发布内容，但直到2020年新冠疫情封锁期间，这位俄亥俄州辛辛那提的本土主播才真正投身于全职直播。对足球的热爱，尤其是对自己最喜欢的球员克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多的崇拜，塑造了他最早的直播内容——他会一边玩《FIFA》与《NBA 2K》，一边让观众陪看。正是那种夹杂尖叫、时常带争议的作秀行为，让他一度迅速走红，但随着时间推移，Speed 逐渐摆脱了传统主播的“操作手册”。他的品牌前景早已不局限于房间里的这张床边舞台。

paragraphs.en: As his audience expanded, so did his mission. Streamers succeed by getting fans to sit and watch. Speed doesn’t. The goal, he tells me, is to become a conduit of inspiration to his nearly 55 million subscribers. When he started engaging with people beyond his computer screen, it gave him purpose. He wants his audience to have that too. So in 2024 he leveled up and started recording travelogues from various countries, turning his stream into a global group chat where he introduced his young audience to different cultures. He has raced a cheetah in South Africa. Sparred with Manny Pacquiao in the Philippines. Was mobbed by fans in Jamaica and Barbados. While in Indonesia, he hit 1 million concurrent live viewers, making him the first English-speaking streamer since the Chandrayaan-3 moon landing to break the record on YouTube. Last year, he embarked on a monthlong, nonstop livestream tour across 25 US states, never once turning the camera off, even while asleep.
paragraphs.zh: 随着观众规模的扩大，他的使命也同步扩大。主播通常依靠让粉丝“坐下来看”来成功，而Speed做不到这一点。正如他告诉我的那样，他的目标是成为一个灵感的传递者，激励他近5500万订阅者。当他开始与电脑屏幕之外的人互动后，这给了他更明确的人生意义；他也希望观众也能有同样的体验。因此，在2024年，他升级了内容形式，开始拍摄世界各地的旅行见闻，把自己的直播变成一个全球群聊，让年轻观众接触不同文化。他在南非与猎豹赛跑，在菲律宾与曼尼·帕奎奥切磋；他在牙买加和巴巴多斯遭遇了粉丝围堵。去年在印度尼西亚，他在直播中吸引了100万同时在线观众，成为自“钱德拉扬三号”月球着陆以来，第一位在YouTube上打破该纪录的英文主播。上个年初，他在美国25个州进行了为期一个月的无间断直播巡演，期间一次也没有关闭过摄像头，甚至在睡觉时也没停过。

paragraphs.en: His rise intersects with a complete transformation in how sports are consumed. Linear television is dead. Streaming services struggle to retain viewers turned off by rising costs. People are their own media platforms now. Few have pulled that transition off with more daring—and reach—than Speed, who is basically inseparable from soccer’s global storytelling apparatus at this point. For generations Z and Alpha, he is practically ESPN.
paragraphs.zh: 他的崛起正与体育消费模式的全面转变交织在一起。传统电视已不再主导。流媒体平台在不断上升的订阅成本下，难以留住观众。如今人人都成了自己的媒体平台。能够更果断、更广泛地完成这一转变的人并不多，而Speed在这方面几乎是无可匹敌的，如今他几乎已经与足球的全球叙事体系水乳交融。对Z世代和Alpha一代来说，他几乎就是ESPN。

paragraphs.en: For this year’s World Cup, Speed is bringing his love of the game to stream from different host cities. FIFA estimates the 2026 cup will draw more than 6 billion total viewers globally across the tournament’s month-plus of play. Speed’s livestreams, no doubt, will add even more eyes to that number.
paragraphs.zh: 在今年的世界杯上，Speed将把他对这项运动的热爱带到不同的主办城市进行直播。国际足联估计，2026年世界杯将在赛事持续一个多月期间吸引全球超过60亿名观众。毫无疑问，Speed的直播会让这个数字再增加更多观看人数。

paragraphs.en: This interview has been edited for length and clarity.
paragraphs.zh: 本文采访内容经过了删减和修订，以便更清晰。

paragraphs.en: JASON PARHAM: You got into the game early, but now everyone wants to be a streamer or a podcast host. Is your level of fame still attainable?
paragraphs.zh: 杰森·帕汉（JASON PARHAM）：你是很早就进入这行的，但现在每个人都想成为主播或播客主持人。你目前的名气水平还是可以达到的吗？

paragraphs.en: ISHOWSPEED: It’s possible. Obviously, I have played a big, big role in this current generation of streaming, but years from now there are going to be people who are better at it than I was. There’s a big history behind it, with YouTube and Twitch, but the industry is still being born.
paragraphs.zh: IShowSpeed：这是有可能的。显然，我在这一代直播潮流中扮演了非常重要的角色，但多年之后，会有人比我当年做得更好。背后有着长期的发展历史，涉及YouTube和Twitch，但这个行业仍处于诞生阶段。

paragraphs.en: What defines your generation?
paragraphs.zh: 是什么定义了你这一代？

paragraphs.en: I am part of the first generation of media streamers. We are the mainstream. I mean, I would say a channel with 50 million subscribers is 100 percent a network. Even a channel with 1 million subs or 500—that’s the reason why streaming is so popular. This industry is a game, but at the end of the day, you can’t beat anybody else. I have to keep continuing to get better with who I am.
paragraphs.zh: 我属于第一代媒体主播。我们是主流。我的意思是，我会说一个拥有5000万订阅者的频道，百分之百就是一个媒体网络。即使一个只有100万或50万订阅者的频道——这就是流媒体受欢迎的原因。这是个游戏，但归根结底，你不能超越任何人。我必须继续努力，在保留我自己的风格下变得更好。

paragraphs.en: Your streams have broken multiple records, and the tours are considered appointment viewing at this point. Is there such a thing as giving fans too much access?
paragraphs.zh: 你的直播已经打破了多项纪录，而且这些巡演现在已经被视为“预约观看”节目了。会不会存在给粉丝“过度接触”的情况？

paragraphs.en: That’s a pretty crazy question to ask me. But it can be about as much access as you want it to be. I’m more of a personal streamer, as raw as it can get, and I want to be as close to the viewers as I can. I want them to be transparent with me and give me advice. But there is a point where it’s like, yeah, that’s personal.
paragraphs.zh: 这确实是个很疯狂的问题。因为接触程度可以按你自己的意愿来设定。我更倾向于做一个尽可能真实的个人主播，而且我希望自己能尽量贴近观众。我希望他们对我坦诚，也向我提建议。但总有一条线，那就是——这儿属于个人隐私。

paragraphs.en: Has that ever worked against you? Social media is known to enable parasocial relationships.
paragraphs.zh: 这是否曾经对你不利？众所周知，社交媒体会催生“拟社会关系”（parasocial relationships）。

paragraphs.en: I encountered a lot of that early in my career, but as I grew I realized that’s what the job comes with. It really, really, really used to bother me when I was younger. People still troll; they still do their thing.
paragraphs.zh: 我在职业生涯早期遇到过不少这样的情况，但我逐渐成长后明白，这正是这份职业附带的现实。年轻时，这确实、确实、真的让我很困扰。人们仍然会“喷子化”；他们还是照样在做他们的事。

paragraphs.en: I’m curious how you think about access and what it means to let people in, because there was a moment during your recent Caribbean tour this spring where you collapsed live on stream.
paragraphs.zh: 我很好奇你如何看待“进入与开放”这个问题，以及让别人“参与进来”意味着什么，因为你今年春天在加勒比巡回直播时，确实有一刻是当场在直播里“倒下”的。

paragraphs.en: See, the thing about me, I never really lose energy. But I am human. My muscles got very tired. But I was still conscious. It was a weird feeling, because I could still hear everything, I knew what was going on, but I couldn’t open up my eyes or move my body. I was just like—yup, GGs. [GGs, or good game, is streamer slang meant to show good sportsmanship or respect to other players.] I was fine though. I just needed to chill. I was very over-fatigued. When you stream five countries in one day, something’s bound to happen. If anything, I was surprised that that was the first time it was really happening.
paragraphs.zh: 你看，就我自己而言，我几乎从不真正“掉电”。但我毕竟是人。我的肌肉累得很厉害。不过我还是清醒的。那种感觉很奇怪，因为我还能听见一切、明白发生了什么，但就是不能睁开眼睛，也动不了身体。我当时就觉得——没错，GGs。 [这里的“GGs”是主播用语，意思是“good game”，用来表示对其他玩家的体育精神或尊重。] 但我没事，我只是需要放松一下。我太过劳累了。你一天里连着直播五个国家，肯定会发生点什么。就算如何，我反而惊讶的是，这第一次真的真的发生了。

paragraphs.en: What makes you say that?
paragraphs.zh: 你怎么会这么说？

paragraphs.en: People don’t understand how much skill it takes. My form of streaming is most definitely a sport.
paragraphs.zh: 人们不理解这其中需要付出多少技巧。我的直播方式绝对算是一种体育运动。

paragraphs.en: I do six- to eight-hour streams. A whole day of doing activities. I may just look like I’m walking or meeting with people, but it all has an art to it. I might randomly race somebody out of nowhere in the middle of the street. Then I’ll try their national dish. The food is not even settled, then boom, I might race someone again. I’m truly doing activities the whole time, and my body has to be agile and fast. I burn so many calories. Per stream, I probably burn around 3,000 to 5,000 calories. So I’m eating like 7,000 calories a stream. It’s a whole performance.
paragraphs.zh: 我会开 6 到 8 个小时的直播，相当于整整一天都在做活动。我看起来可能只是随便走走、和别人见个面，但其实每一步都有设计。中间可能会突然在大街上和某人来一场赛跑，然后我会去尝试他们的国家菜。饭还没怎么吃下去，转眼又可能再去跟别人赛跑。我整个直播过程中都在做事，身体必须保持灵活和快速。我会消耗大量热量。每场直播我大概会燃烧 3000 到 5000 卡路里，所以我每场要吃大约 7000 卡路里。这完全是一场表演。

paragraphs.en: What are you most excited about for the World Cup?
paragraphs.zh: 你对这届世界杯最兴奋的是什么？

paragraphs.en: The feeling. The World Cup always brings you a feeling. The last World Cup that I experienced [in 2022] had so many emotions. It just brings out a different side of people.
paragraphs.zh: 这份感觉。世界杯总会带来一种情绪。回想我经历的上一届世界杯（2022 年），里面有太多情感。它会把人性里不同的一面都引出来。

paragraphs.en: Soccer has never really taken off in the US. You’ll likely have one of the most-watched streams during the tournament. Is it an opportunity to expose more Americans to the sport?
paragraphs.zh: 足球在美国从来没有真正起势。你很可能会在这届赛事里拿下收视最高的直播之一。那会不会是一个让更多美国人接触这项运动的机会？

paragraphs.en: Yeah. But the World Cup being in America already exposes so much. A lot of Americans are going to really fall in love with the game. If we didn’t have American football, I think it would be way more popular.
paragraphs.zh: 是的。不过世界杯在美国举办本身就暴露了很多问题。很多美国人会真心爱上这项运动。如果我们没有美式橄榄球，我想它会更受欢迎得多。

paragraphs.en: Here’s WIRED’s complete guide to the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
paragraphs.zh: 这是《WIRED》为2026年FIFA世界杯带来的完整指南。

paragraphs.en: I fell in love with European football because it’s very accessible, like the art of it. The whole sport of football is generally just beautiful. A lot of people in America underrate European football. I do try to be an advocate for it.
paragraphs.zh: 我爱上欧洲足球，是因为它很容易理解，像艺术一样。整项足球运动通常都非常美。很多美国人低估了欧洲足球。我确实在尽量为它发声。

paragraphs.en: Is your responsibility as a streamer something you think about?
paragraphs.zh: 你会考虑自己作为主播的职责吗？

paragraphs.en: My responsibility as a streamer is to always deliver my root message, and my root message is joy. People always yearn for that. We all have different lives, and everybody’s going through something with all the problems in the world, but people tend to forget that they can get through it. You know what I’m saying? I feel like I can be the symbol for them to get through it.
paragraphs.zh: 作为一个主播，我的职责始终是传递我最核心的信息，我的核心信息就是快乐。人们总是渴望这一点。我们的生活都不一样，每个人都在面对生活中的各种问题，但人们往往会忘记，他们其实可以走过这些难关。你明白我的意思吗？我觉得自己可以成为他们度过这些困难的象征。

paragraphs.en: Do you mean problems in the world politically?
paragraphs.zh: 你的意思是世界上政治方面的问题吗？

paragraphs.en: Yes. And not just joy, confidence. They need meaning.
paragraphs.zh: 是的。不只是快乐，还有自信。他们需要意义。

paragraphs.en: Where does that need to give people confidence and joy come from?
paragraphs.zh: 那种让人拥有自信和快乐的需求从哪里来？

paragraphs.en: It may sound crazy, but I was at a bad point in my life, and I came across the anime One Piece. I was inspired by [the main character] Luffy. I don’t care that it’s an anime or what people think of it. It really helped me through the rough times that I had during that time of my life.
paragraphs.zh: 这听起来可能很疯狂，但我当时人生走到一个很糟糕的阶段，后来我接触了《海贼王》。我被主角路飞所启发。我不在乎它是动漫，也不在乎别人怎么评价它。它真的帮助我度过了我人生中那段艰难时期。

paragraphs.en: What was going on at that point?
paragraphs.zh: 那时到底发生了什么？

paragraphs.en: This was during 2020. I was 15. It’s quarantine. I’m not doing anything with my life. Like, nothing. I’m used to going to school every day, kicking it with my friends, and going to football practice. I felt like I had a life. I was stuck in the house. I wasn’t doing anything, just playing video games all day. I wasn’t streaming at the time. I was lost. I was depressed. I had a lot going on at home, personally. People don’t really understand, but I came from a rough background. I didn’t grow up with a lot of stuff. I had to really work from literally ground zero.
paragraphs.zh: 那是在2020年。我那时15岁。那是封城隔离的时期。我人生中没有在做任何事。真的什么都没做。我习惯了每天去上学、和朋友在一起、去踢足球训练。我本以为自己有了生活，但我被困在家里。我没有做任何事，就整天玩电子游戏。那时候我也没在直播。我的状态很迷茫。我有抑郁。家里也有很多事情缠绕着我，个人层面的。别人不太能真正理解，但我的成长环境并不轻松。我没在富足中长大。后来我真的要从“零起点”开始拼命努力。

paragraphs.en: Is streaming from space still the ultimate goal, or has that changed?
paragraphs.zh: 从太空里直播还是终极目标，还是说这个目标已经改变了？

paragraphs.en: I actually wanted to be an astronaut when I was younger. I was very into it in high school, when I was learning about the atmosphere. I’ve always had this explorer side of me, so it’s funny how that is coming to life now through my YouTube career. Elon Musk, the way his mind works, he’s a smart guy. And as of right now, he can make that happen because he has the tech for it. Like, I would drop everything I’m doing right now and go.
paragraphs.zh: 我年轻的时候其实想当宇航员。高中时我对它很感兴趣，那时我正在学习大气层相关内容。我一直都有一股探险家的性格，所以看到这种性格现在通过我的 YouTube 职业生涯被重新点亮，真是有点有趣。伊隆·马斯克啊，他那种思维方式，确实很聪明。并且就目前来说，他能够把这件事实现，因为他拥有实现它的技术。换句话说，我现在会立刻放下手头正在做的一切直接出发。

paragraphs.en: So there’s no moral dilemma in being associated with Elon Musk, especially since what you do is all about bringing people together? He is someone who has promoted misinformation, allowed harassment to run wild on X, and fast-tracked the erosion of the US government with DOGE.
paragraphs.zh: 那么，和伊隆·马斯克站在一起，真的不存在道德困境吗？尤其是你做的事情本质上就是把人们聚在一起的时候更是如此？他是一个传播错误信息的人，纵容了 X 平台上的骚扰泛滥，还通过 DOGE 推进了美国政府的进一步瓦解。

paragraphs.en: I’m not going to answer that. I’m not going to speak on it.
paragraphs.zh: 我不打算回答这个问题。我不打算在这个问题上表态。

paragraphs.en: But you haven’t shied away from controversy in the past. Often celebrities and influencers have a tendency to keep a neutral stance on polarizing issues to protect their brand. Yet you’ve been a vocal supporter of Palestine—why speak out and say “free Palestine”?
paragraphs.zh: 但你过去并没有回避过争议。很多明星和网红面对两极化议题时往往倾向于保持中立，以保护自己的个人品牌。然而你却是巴勒斯坦问题上非常公开的支持者——为什么要站出来说“free Palestine”呢？

paragraphs.en: That’s just as simple as it gets. My fans who watch me, they’re Muslim. When I first found out, I was in London, and my friend Chunkz told me about what was going on [with Palestine]. I was like, yo, this isn’t good. And I started speaking out and being an advocate about that.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是最直接、最简单的。看我的粉丝里，有些是穆斯林。当我第一次知道这件事时，我在伦敦，我的朋友 Chunkz 告诉我巴勒斯坦发生了什么。我当时想：这太不对了。于是我开始发声，并为此发起倡导。

paragraphs.en: Will you use your platform to engage other issues?
paragraphs.zh: 你会利用你的平台去参与其他议题吗？

paragraphs.en: I can’t tell. As the person who I am right now, I love to inspire people. My career is going to change throughout the years—I don’t know what I’m going to do later—but bro, I always want my messages to be about fun, joy, and confidence, because that’s who I am at the end of the day.
paragraphs.zh: 我说不准。以现在的我来说，我喜欢激励人们。我的职业生涯会在这些年不断变化——我也不知道以后会做什么——但兄弟，核心始终是，我希望我的表达总是围绕快乐、喜悦和自信，因为这才是我最终的样子。

paragraphs.en: Do you ever worry an AI streamer might replace you one day?
paragraphs.zh: 你是否担心有一天会被 AI 直播主取代？

paragraphs.en: No. Obviously, it’s moving at a rapid pace, and we don’t know how it’s going to evolve. We’re already in a whole new era of the internet, and people don’t even realize it.
paragraphs.zh: 不。显然，它发展得非常快，我们也不知道未来会怎样演变。我们已经走进了一个全新的互联网时代，而很多人甚至还没意识到这一点。

paragraphs.en: People realize it. The hard part is trying to keep pace with all the rapid change.
paragraphs.zh: 人们是意识到这一点的。难点在于，如何跟上这种快速变化的节奏。

paragraphs.en: There are so many AI videos and so much AI that’s uprooting everything. It’s scary. It’s one of those things where you just have to adapt. That’s how humans have been surviving. We adapted by being as creative and innovative as we can. But I am not worried, because people are always going to want a real, organic connection.
paragraphs.zh: 现在有太多AI视频，太多AI正在颠覆一切。这很可怕。这就是那种你只能被迫去适应的事。人类之所以能够生存下去，就是靠这种适应。我们靠尽可能地创新和创造来适应。但我并不担心，因为人们始终会想要真实的、有机的连接。

paragraphs.en: We are going to really miss that. So it’s always going to be there, lingering. No matter how much AI evolves, people are still going to want that human connection that we were born with. We’re always going to return to that.
paragraphs.zh: 我们一定会很想念那种东西。所以它总会一直存在、始终萦绕着我们。无论AI发展到何种程度，人类依旧会追求我们与生俱来的那种人际连接。我们总会回到那里。

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎告诉我们你对这篇文章的看法。请写信给编辑部，邮箱是[email protected]。
